s2.ptxt: Using a brake drum gauge or an equivalent tool, measure the inside diameter of the disc.
If the inside diameter is more than the maximum, replace the disc.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PARKING BRAKE SHOE LINING THICKNESS

s2.ptxt: Using a ruler, measure the thickness of the shoe lining.
If the lining thickness is less than the minimum, or if there is severe or uneven wear, replace the brake shoe.

atten3.ptxt: Always replace both the right and left parking brake shoes together.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT BRAKE DISC AND PARKING BRAKE SHOE LINING FOR PROPER CONTACT

s2.ptxt: Apply chalk to the inside surface of the disc, then grind down the brake shoe lining to fit the disc.
If the contact between the disc and the brake shoe lining is improper, repair it using a brake shoe grinder or replace the brake shoe.
